hyperedges.label: Booking Creation Validation and Slot Check Pipeline

nodes.label: Dual Authentication Strategy (JWT + API Key)
nodes.rationale: Dashboard operations require JWT (tenant-issued Bearer token, 7d expiry); public-facing booking/availability endpoints use static API keys via x-api-key header. This separation allows embedding the API key in client widgets without exposing the admin credential.

nodes.label: Multi-Tenant SaaS Architecture
nodes.rationale: Each business (tenant) owns isolated data; tenantId is always included in Mongoose queries to enforce ownership at the DB layer rather than post-fetch filtering in application code.

nodes.label: Fail-Fast Environment Validation
nodes.rationale: Zod validates all required env vars at module load time so misconfigured deployments crash immediately with a clear error rather than failing silently at runtime when a missing var is first accessed.

nodes.label: Query-Level Tenant Data Scoping
nodes.rationale: Ownership is enforced at the database query level (tenantId in every find/update filter) rather than post-fetch filtering, preventing cross-tenant data leaks even if middleware is bypassed.

nodes.label: Email Errors Must Not Crash Server
nodes.norm_label: email errors must not crash server

links.source_location: Appointment Types group: CRUD + GET availability
createTenant hashes password+generates apiKey at creation; authController verifies password+issues JWT at login — complementary credential lifecycle steps
line 24: app.listen(0) — binds to ephemeral port to verify HTTP layer
line 20: env.MONGO_URL consumed by connectDB call
req.tenant injected by apiKeyAuth is consumed in createBooking and cancelBooking (API key paths)
req.tenant injected by jwtAuth consumed in getMe, updateMe, addBlockedTime, removeBlockedTime
both inject req.tenant (apiKeyAuth line 27; jwtAuth line 41) — shared contract with downstream controllers
both implement fail-fast checks: env.js at module load, verify-build.js as an explicit pre-deploy probe
